attacking_style: Explosive late attacks in final 1-2km of summit finishes, capitalizing on hesitation from GC-focused teams. Prefers attacking established trains rather than initiating from distance.
team_tactics: Functions as protected leader but lacks deep mountain domestique support to control race tempo. Relies on positioning himself within rival team trains rather than deploying own team.
terrain_preference: Summit finishes on extended climbs where seated climbing efficiency becomes decisive. Struggles when bad luck (mechanicals, crashes) strikes during critical selection phases.
vulnerability: Highly susceptible to mechanical issues and crashes during selection moments; lacks team depth to recover position. Bad luck has repeatedly cost GC placement (2019, 2020 Vuelta).
key_patterns: Attacks within final kilometer of climbs when positioned on rival trains (2021 Catalunya win)
Demonstrates superior seated climbing efficiency in decisive moments (2018 Giro, 2021 Catalunya)
Mechanical problems and crashes repeatedly disrupt GC campaigns during critical phases (2019, 2020 Vuelta)
Long gaps between wins but climbing level resurfaces when form peaks (2019-2021 gap)
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 Catalunya win after two-year drought suggests resurgent climbing form, positioning BikeExchange with credible GC option
